{"id":"project-LICHEN-jr0c","title":"Dead code warnings in no_std build - lollipop functions not feature-gated","description":"File: rust/lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs
Lines: 29-48

File: rust/lichen-rpl/src/routing.rs  
Lines: 24-44

When building without the `std` feature, several constants and functions are unused:
```
warning: constant `LOLLIPOP_CIRCULAR_BIT` is never used
  --> lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs:29
warning: constant `LOLLIPOP_SEQUENCE_WINDOW` is never used  
  --> lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs:30
warning: function `version_is_newer` is never used
  --> lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs:32
warning: constant `LOLLIPOP_CIRCULAR_BIT` is never used
  --> lichen-rpl/src/routing.rs:24
warning: constant `LOLLIPOP_SEQUENCE_WINDOW` is never used
  --> lichen-rpl/src/routing.rs:25
```

Impact:
1. Code bloat in no_std builds (linker may still include dead code)
2. Compiler warnings make it harder to spot real issues
3. The `seq_is_newer` function in routing.rs is properly gated with #[cfg(feature = "std")] but the constants are not

Fix: Add `#[cfg(feature = "std")]` to the constants and helper functions:
```rust
#[cfg(feature = "std")]
const LOLLIPOP_CIRCULAR_BIT: u8 = 128;
#[cfg(feature = "std")]
const LOLLIPOP_SEQUENCE_WINDOW: u8 = 16;

#[cfg(feature = "std")]
fn version_is_newer(new_ver: u8, old_ver: u8) -> bool { ... }
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:41:42.713883Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:41:42.713919Z"}
